name: Lakarri and Egulbati Circular from Alzuza
name_original: Irotz - Lakarri - Egulbati – circular desde Alzuza
description: This difficult 19.2-mile mountain biking route, Irotz - Lakarri - Egulbati - circular from Alzuza, offers varied terrain and scenic views.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Egüés offer a diverse natural environment within the metropolitan area of Pamplona, Navarre. The region features varied terrain, including Pyrenean foothills with significant elevation changes, dense Scots pine forests, and expansive river valleys. These landscapes provide a mix of challenging ascents and descents, alongside more gentle routes through agricultural plains and along riverbanks. The area's distinct orographic zones ensure a wide range of mountain biking experiences. Best mountain bike trails in Egüés The most popular mountain bike trail is Lakarri and Egulbati Circular from Alzuza, a 19.2 miles (31.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 27 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain through Pyrenean topography. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is View of Ezkoriz from Aranguren loop from Mendillorri, a moderate 18.7 miles (30.1 km) path. This route offers scenic views and varied terrain, including sections through the central valley. Local mountain bikers also love the View of Balsa de Ezkotitz – View of the Elorz Valley loop from Sarriguren, a 26.9 miles (43.3 km) trail leading through diverse landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 23 minutes. Mountain biking around Egüés is defined by Pyrenean foothills, dense forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from challenging mountain climbs to less strenuous cross-country rides. The routes in Egüés are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Egüés's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Egüés?
A: Egüés offers a diverse network of over 20 mountain bike trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from easy to difficult, ensuring there's something for every rider.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Egüés?
A: The terrain in Egüés is quite varied. You'll find challenging ascents and descents in the Pyrenean foothills, dense Scots pine forests, and expansive river valleys. The northern areas feature protected natural zones with a mosaic of forests and natural meadows, while the central and southern parts offer gentler slopes and agricultural plains along the Urbi River. This mix provides everything from rugged mountain climbs to less strenuous cross-country rides.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Egüés?
A: The mountain bike routes in Egüés are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the area, often praising the varied terrain and scenic beauty.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Egüés?
A: Yes, Egüés offers several routes suitable for beginners. Out of the 24 available trails, 6 are classified as easy, providing a good starting point for those new to mountain biking or looking for a more relaxed ride. These often follow gentler slopes in the central valley or along riverbanks.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Egüés features 4 difficult mountain bike routes. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989948677/lakarri-and-egulbati-circular-from-alzuza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lakarri and Egulbati Circular from Alzuza&lt;/a&gt;, a 31 km trail with over 1000 meters of elevation gain through Pyrenean topography, taking around 3 hours 27 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or natural monuments to see along the trails?
A: Yes, the Egüés area boasts several natural attractions. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5315216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zokorena Dam&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2595283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Diablozulo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The region also includes protected natural areas like Elía and Egulbati, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5378954" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isabelina Butterfly Micro-reserve&lt;/a&gt;, offering unique natural sights.

Q: What other interesting landmarks can I explore near the mountain bike routes?
A: Beyond natural features, you can discover historical and architectural landmarks. These include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4817230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magdalena Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5135510" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rabies Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4396525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pamplona Cathedral and the Citadel&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5131900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portal de Francia&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3622099" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gazebo in Plaza del Castillo&lt;/a&gt; also offer cultural points of interest.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Egüés?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Egüés are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31859477" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Ezkoriz from Aranguren loop from Mendillorri&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31903136" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Balsa de Ezkotitz – View of the Elorz Valley loop from Sarriguren&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Egüés?
A: Egüés offers good mountain biking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. While the region can experience winter conditions, many trails remain accessible. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon rides are often preferred.

Q: Can I find family-friendly mountain bike trails in Egüés?
A: Yes, the varied terrain of Egüés includes gentler routes suitable for families. The flatter areas and riverbanks in the central and southern parts of the valley offer less strenuous options, perfect for cross-country biking with children. Look for trails classified as 'easy' for the most family-friendly experiences.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the mountain bike trails in Egüés?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many outdoor trails in the Egüés region, especially in natural areas. However, it's always recommended to keep them under control, on a leash where required, and to check for specific local regulations or signage on individual routes or protected areas to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

Q: What are the parking options for mountain bikers in Egüés?
A: Many trailheads in and around Egüés, particularly those starting from towns like Alzuza, Mendillorri, or Sarriguren, offer convenient parking facilities. It's advisable to check the specific starting point of your chosen route on komoot for detailed parking information.

Q: Is public transport available to reach mountain bike trailheads in Egüés?
A: Egüés is located within the metropolitan area of Pamplona, so public transport options, including bus services, are available to reach various points within the valley. Some routes may start directly from villages or towns accessible by public transport, making it possible to reach trailheads without a car.
tips: Very cool and entertaining path with beautiful views, especially on the descent from the top of San Miguel towards the valley of the Arga river.
From the repeater you can go down between the pines. It is a very technical descent due to the poor condition of the road, but it is more fun than going down the gravel road.

Hard and demanding climb to reach the top of Lakarri at 1046 masl. The effort undoubtedly has its rewards to be able to enjoy the beautiful views from up here. In autumn you will have an amazing landscape with the varied colors of the trees in the valley.
If you have time, it is worth climbing to the top of Lakarri, the views are spectacular.
The top of Egulbati at 933 meters above sea level offers some beautiful views. You can combine the climb up to here combining with the top of Lakarri, another quintessential viewpoint in the area.

faqs.question: Is the Irotz - Lakarri - Egulbati circular mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this is a difficult mountain bike route requiring very good fitness and advanced riding skills. You should be prepared for very steep ascents, with some sections likely requiring you to dismount and push your bike.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route features varied and rugged terrain, including demanding ascents and descents through mountain meadows, heathland, and native forests. You might also encounter karst formations typical of the Navarra region.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable spots. The El Cordal segment is particularly known for its entertaining path and beautiful views, especially during the descent towards the Arga River valley. Other highlights include the Irotz Repeater, the Lakarri summit, the Lakarri Pass, and the Egulbati Summit.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Alzuza. While specific parking details aren't provided, you would typically look for public parking areas within the village of Alzuza to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many trails in the region are dog-friendly, given the route's difficult classification and the potential for steep, technical sections, it might not be ideal for all dogs. If you do bring your dog, ensure they are fit enough for the demanding terrain and always keep them on a leash.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The Navarra region experiences varied weather. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential snow or heavy rains in winter. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: No, typically you do not need special permits or have to pay entrance fees to ride this mountain bike route. It is generally open for public access.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of other paths, including the Tour of the Pamplona Basin, Alzuza, and The Pathway of the Arga river.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or water sources near the trail?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Alzuza, where you might find some basic amenities. However, given the challenging and potentially remote nature of parts of the trail, it's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks for the entire duration, as on-route facilities are unlikely.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this circular route?
faqs.answer: Given its difficult rating and duration, you should bring plenty of water, energy-rich snacks, a repair kit for your bike (including spare tubes), a first-aid kit, appropriate protective gear, and navigation tools. Wearing layers is also recommended due to potential elevation changes and varied weather.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: On average, completing this challenging 31 km route takes approximately 3 hours and 27 minutes. However, your actual time may vary depending on your fitness level, riding speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the views.
